texto: I don't like gratuitous violence. I don't like the 'Saw' movies. I don't like the 'Hostel' movies. I don't like anything that is violence for violence's sake.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michael Biehn: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't like gratuitous violence. I don't like the 'Saw' movies. I don't like the 'Hostel' movies…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, violence, anything pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de anything e like.

texto: I saw 'Taxi Driver,' and 'Taxi Driver' kind of saved my life. The scene where Robert De Niro is looking at himself in the mirror saying, 'You talkin' to me? You talkin' to me? Who the hell else are you talkin' to?' That's the scene that changed my life by changing my attitude about acting.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Michael Biehn que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “I saw 'Taxi Driver,' and 'Taxi Driver' kind of saved my life. The scene where Robert De Niro is l…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre vida, mirror, me, com foco em mudanca e mirror.

Who the hell else are you talkin' to?' That's the scene that changed my life by changing my attitude about acting.

texto: Johnny Ringo to me was just the best antagonist that I've ever played, because I played him as a guy who has a death wish and had done everything that he wanted in life. As far as he was concerned, a gun fight was about as exciting as it was going to get.
explicacao: Citação de Michael Biehn sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Johnny Ringo to me was just the best antagonist that I've ever played, because I played him as a …” articula motivacao, vida, inspiracional em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como luto e inspiracao.
